title: rosa rubiginosa var. flagellaris
intro: Rosa rubiginosa var. flagellaris, also known as the Downy Rose or Flagellated Dwarf Rose, is a rare and lesser-known variety of rose native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Rosa rubiginosa var. flagellaris are believed to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a potential remedy for various health concerns. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
